title: 10 best herbal essential oils for grey hair
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for grey hair are plant-based extracts that help to reduce and reverse grey hair by promoting melanin production, improving scalp health, and enhancing hair growth. The best herbal essential oils for grey hair include Rosmarinus officinalis, Cassia auriculata, Curcuma longa, Aloe barbadensis, Santalum album, Lavandula angustifolia, Urtica dioica, Avena sativa, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Bacopa monnieri. If left untreated, grey hair can lead to low self-esteem, social withdrawal, and decreased confidence, negatively impacting one's life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with grey hair because of its remarkable antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to stimulate hair growth, improve scalp circulation, and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to graying. Additionally, tea tree oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help prevent dandruff and other scalp conditions that may accelerate hair whitening. By using a shampoo or conditioner containing tea tree essential oils, individuals can potentially slow down the graying process and promote healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tea Tree Oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which may be associated with grey hair.
Stimulates blood circulation: Tea Tree Oil can stimulate blood circulation to the scalp, which can promote healthy hair growth and potentially slow down or even reverse grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, tea tree roots help combat fungal infections that can cause premature graying.
Leaves: Abundant in flavonoids and terpenes, tea tree leaves promote healthy scalp circulation, which can improve hair growth and reduce gray hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the foliage.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and impurities gently.
Steam-distill the washed leaves in a copper or stainless steel alembic for 4-6 hours patiently.
Monitor and record the distillation process closely as essential oil yields peak at optimal temperatures carefully.
Filter and bottle the resulting melaleuca alternifolia essential oil immediately after distillation with precision.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with grey hair because of its remarkable ability to stimulate blood flow and circulation to the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in rosemary oil also help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to premature graying of hair. Additionally, rosemary oil's natural astringent properties help to strengthen hair follicles, leading to thicker, darker, and shinier locks. Regular use of rosemary essential oils has been shown to promote a natural pigmentation process, resulting in reduced grey hair and a more youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress, which can cause premature graying and hair loss, thus promoting healthy hair growth.

Improves Circulation: Rosemary essential oil's stimulant properties improve blood circulation to the scalp, which helps to nourish and oxygenate the hair follicles, reducing grey hair and promoting a healthy, natural hair color.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to grey hair and hair loss, thus promoting a healthier scalp environment for hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used in aromatherapy to stimulate blood circulation and promote healthy hair growth, making them an effective choice for combating grey hair.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation on the scalp, contributing to premature graying of hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves at dawn or late evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried rosemary leaves using a distillation apparatus and 300ml of water for 60 minutes.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle.
Store the filtered rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with grey hair because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote healthy scalp and hair follicles. The oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties also help to combat dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp issues that can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, thyme's stimulating properties improve blood circulation, which nourishes the hair roots and encourages natural pigmentation. As a result, using thyme essential oils can help to reverse or slow down the graying process, promoting thicker, healthier, and more vibrant-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant property helps protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature greying.
Stimulant property: The stimulant property of thyme essential oil helps increase blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially reducing grey hair.

Astringent property: Thyme essential oil's astringent property helps to constrict the pores on the scalp, which can help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for hair growth, potentially slowing down or reversing grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting hair growth and reducing grey hair.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes, which help to nourish the scalp, promote hair health, and reduce inflammation that can contribute to grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when they are most potent and rich in bioactive compounds.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or until completely dry.
Grind 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Steam distill the powdered thymus vulgaris in a still apparatus with 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with grey hair because of its natural ability to promote healthy hair growth, reduce oxidative stress, and improve circulation. The oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties also help to soothe an itchy scalp, which can contribute to premature graying. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature greying and thinning.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of English lavender essential oil may help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to grey hair and promote healthy hair growth.
Antimicrobial properties: English lavender essential oil's antimicrobial properties may help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause scalp irritation and accelerate grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and saponins, which can help promote hair growth and reduce graying.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that can improve scalp circulation and promote healthy hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of 100 grams at optimal blooming time for maximum oil yield.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out on paper towels and allowing air circulation for a minimum of 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using 100 milliliters of water per 50 grams of material for a duration of two hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil obtained during distillation to remove impurities and separate it from the hydrosol, yielding approximately 3-5% oil concentration.
Transfer the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool, dry place for up to six months while maintaining its potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality santalum album leaves from organic farms or your own garden at dawn on dry days.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 60°c for 12 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill 100g of dried santalum album leaves with 500ml of water in a stainless steel still for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Use the freshly extracted santalum album essential oil within 6 months, applying 2-3 drops to the scalp twice daily for 3 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for several days.
Use a high-quality steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried calendula flowers at a temperature of 100°c.
Mix the distilled essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:4 and store it in amber glass bottles for up to six months.
Use a dropper bottle to apply the hair treatment blend three times a week to the scalp massaging gently for five minutes each time.
Perform a gentle shampoo using a mild sulfate-free cleanser followed by a hydrating conditioner after applying the calendula essential oil hair treatment for optimal results within four weeks.

Leave the mixture to infuse for at least 24 hours allowing natural processes to unfold.

Combine the resulting essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:10 ratio carefully.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus peel from organic oranges or mandarins in the morning.
Dry the harvested citrus peel for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.

Mix the extracted essential oil with 10% jojoba oil and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 30 days.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth and discard any solids before using the herbal citrus x aurantium essential oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat grey hair with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of lavender, rosemary, and chamomile oils. remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with grey hair because it promotes scalp health and improves circulation, which can contribute to the reduction of grey hair. Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, potentially promoting hair growth and reducing graying.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary source of essential oils for Rosmarinus officinalis, as they contain the highest concentration of rosmarinic acid, a compound that helps to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy hair pigmentation.

Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to create a more delicate and soothing essential oil, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, contributing to a more youthful and vibrant appearance.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain a higher percentage of camphor, a compound that helps to stimulate blood flow and promote a healthy scalp, which can help to slow down the graying process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when the plant is at its peak fragrance and potency.
Steam distill 50 grams of rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in a 1-liter still at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it away from light and heat.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh cassia auriculata leaves when the plant is in full bloom.

Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of curcumin, which has been shown to promote hair growth and reduce graying.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature curcuma longa rhizomes with a sharp knife or sickle after the plant has finished flowering.
Clean the rhizomes in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned rhizomes in a still with a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus in a glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with grey hair because it contains aloin, a compound that has been shown to promote hair growth and reduce grey hair by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme tyrosinase, which contributes to the formation of melanin. The oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to promote healthy scalp conditions that are conducive to hair growth. remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin has been found to promote melanin production and reduce the appearance of grey hair by stimulating the hair follicles and promoting the growth of new hair.

Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a healthy scalp environment, potentially slowing down the greying process.
Saponins: Saponins have been found to improve blood circulation to the scalp and stimulate the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of aloin, which helps to reduce dandruff and promote healthy hair growth.
Stems: The stems are used as they contain aloe-emodin, a compound that has been shown to help in reducing greying of hair by stimulating the hair follicles.
Roots: The roots are used as they contain a compound called acemannan, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help in promoting hair growth and reducing grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature leaves from the aloe barbadensis plant in the early morning when the gel is most abundant.
Remove the outer skin of the leaves and extract 200 grams of the clear gel-like substance from the inside.
Add 100 grams of distilled water to the gel and mix well for 10 minutes using a hand blender.
Heat the mixture at 40-50 degrees celsius for 30 minutes using a double boiler to facilitate separation of the oil.
Filter the oil through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass bottle to remove any impurities and store.

Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.

Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain a significant amount of antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to help reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy hair growth, potentially addressing grey hair.

Roots: Urtica dioica roots are also used to make essential oils due to their potential to stimulate hair growth and reduce graying, possibly due to their rich content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers early in the morning to ensure optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried urtica dioica plant material at 100-110 degrees celsius to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water through decantation or centrifugation within 24 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and ensure clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with grey hair because it stimulates blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The oil's rich antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage and stress, which can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, Avena sativa is believed to nourish and strengthen the hair, helping to restore its natural color and vibrancy. It is often used in aromatherapy to promote overall hair health and well-being. Regular use may help to reduce the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: It helps by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which may contribute to the graying of hair.
Avenasterol: It helps by promoting the health and pigmentation of hair follicles, leading to a reduction in grey hair.
β-Sitosterol: It helps by reducing inflammation and promoting hair growth, which can help to slow down or reverse the graying of hair.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of fatty acids and other compounds that may help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.

Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are used to make essential oils that may help stimulate hair growth, improve scalp health, and reduce grey hair due to their rich content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa are used to make essential oils that may help reduce stress, promote relaxation, and improve overall hair health, which can contribute to a reduction in grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of avena sativa flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers at 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil, commonly known as tea tree oil, contains compounds that stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to maintain a balanced scalp environment, which is essential for preventing the formation of grey hair. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil may help to reduce or delay the onset of grey hair.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole helps reduce inflammation and promotes healthy scalp conditions, which can contribute to a reduction in premature grey hair.

α-Terpineol: α-Terpineol has antioxidant properties that help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially reducing the appearance of grey hair.
Cymene: Cymene has antiseptic and antioxidant properties that help maintain a healthy scalp environment, reduce inflammation, and promote hair growth, which can lead to a reduction in grey hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for promoting hair growth and reducing grey hair.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to premature greying of hair.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia have been traditionally used to treat various scalp issues, including dandruff and itchiness, which can contribute to grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the early morning when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 14 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with a 1:5 leaf to water ratio for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage.
Store the essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months to preserve its quality and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri essential oils helps with grey hair because of its potential to promote hair growth and improve scalp health. The oil's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that damage hair follicles and cause premature graying. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri contains bacosides, which may enhance the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. By maintaining a healthy scalp and promoting melanin production, Bacopa monnieri essential oils may help delay or even reverse grey hair, promoting a more youthful and vibrant appearance.
remedy_constituents: Bacoside: Helps with grey hair by promoting the health and growth of hair follicles, possibly due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Bacosterol: Contributes to the reduction of grey hair by protecting the hair follicles from oxidative stress and promoting the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color.
Bacoside A: May help with grey hair by enhancing the activity of enzymes involved in the synthesis of melanin, potentially leading to a reduction in grey hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to create essential oils that promote hair growth and reduce greying due to its antioxidant properties.
Stems: Utilized in essential oils to help stimulate hair follicles and reduce grey hair due to its ability to promote cellular health.
Roots: Employed in essential oils to address grey hair by enhancing the body's natural ability to produce melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the morning when the plant is at its highest potency level.
Steam distill the plant material at a temperature of 100-150°f for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Use a gentle stirring rod to mix the essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a ratio of 5:95.
